scope: Parameter optimization proposal and prediction contract v2 after 32-match backtest baseline; no betting advice.

input_readiness.reason: data/packets/model_optimization/tactical-volatility-factors-20260622.json has been read and its draw_floor/event_volatility/favorite_ceiling magnitudes are incorporated as tactical-strength inputs while probability shifts remain capped conservatively.

sample_size_policy.reason: Only 32 completed predictions are in the current baseline, with mixed legacy prose and quant-chain formats.
notes: Prefer reallocating scoreline/tail mass before changing 1X2 headline probabilities.
Do not learn team-specific permanent parameters from one match.
Require red-team review before making these defaults permanent.

draw_floor_dynamic.purpose: Prevent low-event strategic draws from being compressed by favorite reputation or market heat.

tactical_factor_alignment.integration_note: The 0.35 tactical magnitude permits normal/strong low-event draw-floor activation, but final probability movement remains capped at +0.06 before red-team review.

score_matrix_method: Reallocate mass from favorite narrow wins and non-favorite low-probability 3+ tails into 0-0, 1-1 and 2-2.
Default split of added draw mass: 0-0 30%, 1-1 55%, 2-2 15%.
If combined xG is above 2.6, reduce 0-0 split to 15% and raise 2-2 split to 25%.
probability_floor_and_adjustment.normalization: After reallocation, renormalize 1X2 and totals from the adjusted score matrix.

counterexamples_and_no_apply: Clear favorite xG >= 2.0 and opponent xG <= 0.65 with strong lineup continuity.
Known red-card, suspension, or defensive injury against the underdog before kickoff.
Favorite has high set-piece mismatch plus confirmed aerial starters.
Match state or standings make a draw materially poor for both teams.
Market is favorite-heavy but local tactical model also gives favorite win probability >= 0.70.
draw_floor_dynamic.overfit_guard: Use as a floor only when multiple triggers co-occur; never add draw mass because prior rounds had many draws in isolation.
draw_floor_dynamic.red_team_review_note: Tactical draw-floor evidence is now incorporated, but red team must still check whether the match actually permits a draw tactically and standings-wise.

event_volatility_layer.purpose: Separate event-driven variance from linear xG adjustments.

tactical_factor_alignment.integration_note: The 0.45 tactical magnitude maps to medium/high variance handling, but the model tail mass shift remains capped at 0.055 normally and 0.06 before red-team approval.

redistribution_rules: Move tail_mass_shift from central scores around each team's Poisson mode into 3+ total-goal outcomes.
If volatility is discipline/collapse driven, favor one-sided 3-0, 4-0, 4-1, 5-1 style tails for the advantaged team.
If volatility is both-teams-open driven, favor 2-2, 3-2, 2-3, 3-3 tails.
If goalkeeper_overperformance is tagged, move mass toward 0 goals for the attacking favorite and raise 0-0/0-1/1-0 as appropriate.
score_matrix_tail_adjustment.normalization: All adjusted score probabilities must sum to 1 including tail_probability.

counterexamples_and_no_apply: Stable low-block match with low card risk and no pressing mismatch.
High technical favorite likely to control tempo after lead rather than keep attacking.
Volatility trigger already fully represented by an injury or lineup xG adjustment.
event_volatility_layer.overfit_guard: Event layer changes distribution shape, not team quality. Do not permanently raise team attack/defense ratings from one volatile result.
event_volatility_layer.red_team_review_note: Tactical volatility factors are incorporated, but red team must audit duplicate counting with red cards, goalkeeper form and set-piece factors already in player_state or matchup tactics.

favorite_ceiling_tail.purpose: Raise large-win exact-score tails when a strong favorite has credible breakthrough and accumulation paths.

tactical_factor_alignment.integration_note: The 0.40 tactical magnitude supports ceiling-tail activation when multiple favorite triggers fire; exact-score tail expansion is still capped at 0.06.

source_of_mass: draw scores with low tactical support
opponent low-probability 2+ goal tails when opponent attack evidence is weak
tail_expansion_method.tail_probability_handling: If 5+ score tail is material, keep explicit tail_probability and add a note that exact-score top5 may not capture all blowout mass.

counterexamples_and_no_apply: Favorite win probability is high mostly from market heat but local xG remains below 1.65.
Favorite has known rotation/minutes restriction for main finishers.
Opponent has strong goalkeeper and compact low-block evidence with low chase exposure.
Draw is strategically valuable for favorite and coach has history of slowing games after lead.
favorite_ceiling_tail.overfit_guard: Apply only to tail, not as a blanket favorite win bump. Review red-team challenge before publishing high-ceiling language.
favorite_ceiling_tail.red_team_review_note: Tactical favorite-ceiling factors are incorporated, but red team must confirm T-75m attacking lineup, bench depth and that market heat is not being used as tactical evidence.

market_calibration_split.purpose: Use markets as signals without letting favorite heat erase local draw or upset tails.

market_direction_signal.meaning: How strongly market supports 1X2 direction after overround removal.
market_direction_signal.use_when: Market, local xG and tactical mismatch point the same way.

market_tail_signal.meaning: What market totals/handicap imply about blowout, low-event, or draw tails.
market_tail_signal.use_when: Totals and handicap lines are available from same source and timestamp.

conflict_rules: If market favorite probability exceeds model by >=0.12 but model has conversion-risk or low-block flags, do not raise favorite direction; record favorite_heat_conflict.
If market and model agree on favorite and total goals, allow small favorite_ceiling_tail adjustment.
If only moneyline is available, do not infer totals or blowout tail.
If source is public article snippet rather than official/same-source snapshot, cap combined market effect at 0.06.

reporting_rules: Separate highest single scoreline from aggregate 1X2 direction.
Always list top5 scorelines; top3 may be derived from top5 for backtest.
Write market source, capture time and overround when market is used.
If official or same-source odds are missing, set odds_implied_probability and model_market_delta to null with not_available_yet note.
If T-75m lineups are missing, mark confidence and betting/risk output as discussion_only_hold.
Do not output betting recommendations in model files.
validation_rules: Poisson 0-0 to 5-5 plus tail sums within [0.995, 1.005].
No placeholder or unfinished drafting residue before final report.

implementation_sequence.name: schema_contract_enforcement
implementation_sequence.action: Apply prediction_contract_v2 to every new prediction before red-team review.

implementation_sequence.name: conservative_parameter_shadow_mode
implementation_sequence.action: For the next 12 to 20 matches, store both base probabilities and v2-adjusted probabilities for backtest comparison.

implementation_sequence.name: red_team_review
implementation_sequence.action: Send this parameter proposal to red-team for overfitting, double-counting and market-misuse review.

implementation_sequence.name: postmatch_backtest_update
implementation_sequence.action: After each completed match, write WDL/top3/Brier/log-loss/xG/total-goal error and parameter trigger outcomes.

quality_notes: This proposal is intentionally conservative because the baseline has only 32 matches and mixed prediction formats.
Tactical volatility factors are incorporated from the complete_partial_backtest_basis packet, but red-team revise/pass review is still required before permanent default use.
Conservative caps remain unchanged: max single parameter probability shift <= 0.06 and max combined pre-red-team probability shift <= 0.10.
No betting advice is included; market treatment is calibration-only.
source_log: 汇总/模型优化执行方案_20260622.md
